title: Build Beads Orphanage: per-project orphan-branch sync (br orphanage)
description:
# Beads Orphanage (br-orphanage) Implementation Plan > **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking. Execution subagents run on **Sonnet** models (user preference). **Goal:** Replace the central-repo sync model with per-project orphan-branch sync (`br orphanage sync`), a curl installer, and a fully transparent passthrough wrapper. **Architecture:** A single bash wrapper (`bin/br`) shadows the real `br` on PATH. Everything outside the `orphanage`/`o` namespace passes through untouched. Sync uses git plumbing (hash-object/mktree/commit-tree) inside the project's own `.git` — no clones, no worktrees. Per-project config lives in the project's git config under `beadsOrphanage.*`. A machine-local index at `~/.local/share/br-orphanage/project-paths` powers `sync --all`. **Tech Stack:** bash (`set -euo pipefail`, shellcheck-clean), git plumbing, the real `br` binary (beads_rust ≥0.2.16) for all issue-tracker behavior. Tests are one end-to-end harness: `tests/run.sh`. **Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-03-br-orphanage-design.md` — read it before starting any task. --- ## File Structure | File | Responsibility | |---|---| | `bin/br` | The wrapper. Full rewrite: dispatch, `orphanage init/target/sync/--version`, plumbing sync engine. | | `install.sh` | Full rewrite: curl-pipe installer with local-dev-mode detection, XDG data dir, rc-file PATH line, version reporting. | | `tests/run.sh` | Full rewrite: sandboxed e2e harness (fake HOME + XDG_DATA_HOME, bare repos as all remotes, real `br` binary). | | `README.md` | Full rewrite (Task 9). | | `.gitignore` | Deleted (Task 9) — the machine-local index no longer lives in this repo. | Conventions used throughout: - All wrapper messages are prefixed `br-orphanage:` (errors/warnings to stderr). - Functions called from `if (subshell)` contexts check every exit status explicitly and `return 1` — `set -e` is inert there (same hardening discipline as the current code; see comments in the existing `bin/br`). - Empty arrays are expanded with the `${arr[@]+"${arr[@]}"}` guard (macOS bash 3.2 + `set -u` safe). - Run the harness with `tests/run.sh` from anywhere. It prints `ALL PASSED`/exit 0 or `FAILED`/exit 1 and a per-assertion `[PASS]`/`[FAIL]` log. "Expected: FAIL with …" below means the harness exits 1 and the named assertion lines show `[FAIL]`. **Every task ends with a commit containing only implementation/test files. Never commit docs/superpowers/ changes together with code (user requirement).** --- ### Task 1: Installer rewrite + new harness skeleton The harness installs the wrapper via `install.sh` (local dev mode) into a fake HOME and exercises the *installed* copy. At the end of this task the old wrapper is still in place; the skeleton only tests installation and passthrough, which the old wrapper already satisfies. **Files:** - Rewrite: `install.sh` - Rewrite: `tests/run.sh` - [ ] **Step 1: Rewrite `tests/run.sh` with the skeleton below (replaces the entire file)** ```bash #!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail # tests/run.sh — end-to-end harness for the Beads Orphanage `br` wrapper. # # Everything runs inside a throwaway `mktemp -d`, cleaned up on exit via # trap. Nothing touches the invoking user's real home directory, data dir, # beads data, shell rc files, or any real remote. # # - install.sh runs in LOCAL DEV MODE into a fake HOME; the INSTALLED # wrapper (never the checkout's bin/br directly) is what tests exercise. # - The REAL br binary from the invoker's PATH provides issue-tracker # behavior; no fakes. # - Bare repos under $WORK stand in for every remote (project origins and # orphan-branch sync targets). # # Run: tests/run.sh (from anywhere; resolves its own path) SELF=$(readlink -f "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}") TESTS_DIR=$(cd "$(dirname "${SELF}")" && pwd) REPO_UNDER_TEST=$(cd "${TESTS_DIR}/.." && pwd) WORK=$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/br-orphanage-test.XXXXXX") # shellcheck disable=SC2329 # invoked indirectly via the EXIT trap below cleanup() { local status=$? rm -rf "${WORK}" exit "${status}" } trap cleanup EXIT # Fake HOME so the real br's user-level config, the installer's rc edits, # and the wrapper's data dir never touch the invoking user's real files. FAKE_HOME="${WORK}/home" mkdir -p "${FAKE_HOME}" export HOME="${FAKE_HOME}" # Pin the data-dir derivation: installer and wrapper honor XDG_DATA_HOME, # and the invoking user may have it set to a real location. export XDG_DATA_HOME="${FAKE_HOME}/.local/share" export GIT_AUTHOR_NAME=test GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL=test@test.invalid export GIT_COMMITTER_NAME=test GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL=test@test.invalid # --- Locate the REAL br binary on the invoker's PATH -------------------------- find_real_br_on_path() { local dir cand local IFS=':' # shellcheck disable=SC2250 # deliberately unbraced/unquoted: word-splits PATH on the IFS=':' set above for dir in $PATH; do [[ -n "${dir}" ]] || continue cand="${dir}/br" [[ -x "${cand}" ]] && [[ -f "${cand}" ]] || continue printf '%s\n' "${cand}" return 0 done return 1 } # shellcheck disable=SC2310 # failure handled explicitly by the || block REAL_BR=$(find_real_br_on_path) || { echo "FATAL: no real 'br' binary found on PATH; install beads_rust first." >&2 exit 1 } REAL_BR_RESOLVED=$(readlink -f "${REAL_BR}") REAL_BR_DIR=$(dirname "${REAL_BR_RESOLVED}") # --- Pass/fail bookkeeping ----------------------------------------------------- PASS_COUNT=0 FAIL_COUNT=0 section() { printf '\n=== %s ===\n' "$1"; } pass() { PASS_COUNT=$((PASS_COUNT + 1)) printf ' [PASS] %s\n' "$1" } fail() { FAIL_COUNT=$((FAIL_COUNT + 1)) printf ' [FAIL] %s\n' "$1" >&2 } assert_eq() { local desc="$1" expected="$2" actual="$3" if [[ "${expected}" == "${actual}" ]]; then pass "${desc}" else fail "${desc} (expected: '${expected}', actual: '${actual}')" fi } assert_true() { local desc="$1" shift if "$@"; then pass "${desc}" else fail "${desc} (command failed: $*)" fi } assert_file_exists() { local desc="$1" path="$2" if [[ -f "${path}" ]]; then pass "${desc}"; else fail "${desc} (missing file: ${path})"; fi } assert_file_absent() { local desc="$1" path="$2" if [[ ! -e "${path}" ]]; then pass "${desc}"; else fail "${desc} (file unexpectedly present: ${path})"; fi } assert_dir_exists() { local desc="$1" path="$2" if [[ -d "${path}" ]]; then pass "${desc}"; else fail "${desc} (missing directory: ${path})"; fi } assert_contains() { local desc="$1" haystack="$2" needle="$3" if [[ "${haystack}" == *"${needle}"* ]]; then pass "${desc}" else fail "${desc} (did not find '${needle}' in output)" fi } # --- Shared helpers ------------------------------------------------------------- # `git rev-parse --git-path

` is relative from a normal repo but absolute # from a linked worktree. Normalize to always-absolute. abs_git_path() { local repo_dir="$1" rel_path="$2" out out=$(cd "${repo_dir}" && git rev-parse --git-path "${rel_path}") case "${out}" in /*) printf '%s\n' "${out}" ;; *) printf '%s/%s\n' "${repo_dir}" "${out}" ;; esac } # Fresh git repo at $1 with an initial commit; optionally a fake bare origin # at $WORK/origins/.git (which doubles as an own-repo sync target). make_project_repo() { local dir="$1" want_origin="${2:-yes}" name="${3:-}" mkdir -p "${dir}" git init -q "${dir}" git -C "${dir}" commit -q --allow-empty -m "initial commit" if [[ "${want_origin}" == "yes" ]]; then local origin_bare="${WORK}/origins/${name}.git" mkdir -p "$(dirname "${origin_bare}")" git init -q --bare "${origin_bare}" git -C "${dir}" remote add origin "${origin_bare}" fi } # --- Setup: run install.sh in local dev mode ------------------------------------ section "Setup: install.sh (local dev mode) into fake HOME" INSTALL_DIR="${XDG_DATA_HOME}/br-orphanage" INSTALLED_BR="${INSTALL_DIR}/bin/br" touch "${FAKE_HOME}/.bashrc" INSTALL_OUT=$("${REPO_UNDER_TEST}/install.sh") assert_contains "installer reports local-checkout install" "${INSTALL_OUT}" "installed from local checkout" assert_file_exists "wrapper installed at data dir" "${INSTALLED_BR}" assert_true "installed wrapper is executable" test -x "${INSTALLED_BR}" assert_contains "installer reports the installed version" "${INSTALL_OUT}" "installed version" assert_true "rc file got the marked PATH line" grep -qF "# br-orphanage" "${FAKE_HOME}/.bashrc" REINSTALL_OUT=$("${REPO_UNDER_TEST}/install.sh") assert_contains "re-install does not duplicate the rc line" "${REINSTALL_OUT}" "already configured" RC_LINE_COUNT=$(grep -cF "# br-orphanage" "${FAKE_HOME}/.bashrc") assert_eq "exactly one marked PATH line after two installs" "1" "${RC_LINE_COUNT}" # Upgrade reporting: fake an older installed version, re-run installer. sed -i.bak 's/^VERSION=".*"$/VERSION="0.0.0"/' "${INSTALLED_BR}" && rm -f "${INSTALLED_BR}.bak" UPGRADE_OUT=$("${REPO_UNDER_TEST}/install.sh") assert_contains "upgrade reports old -> new version" "${UPGRADE_OUT}" "updated 0.0.0 ->" # The wrapper under test comes FIRST on PATH; real binary's dir next so the # wrapper's own PATH scan (skipping itself) finds the real one. export PATH="${INSTALL_DIR}/bin:${REAL_BR_DIR}:${PATH}" assert_eq "PATH resolves 'br' to the installed wrapper" \ "$(readlink -f "${INSTALLED_BR}")" "$(readlink -f "$(command -v br)")" # --- Regression: executable bits tracked in git ---------------------------------- section "Regression: executable bits tracked in git (mode 100755)" BIN_MODE=$(git -C "${REPO_UNDER_TEST}" ls-files -s bin/br | awk '{print $1}') assert_eq "bin/br tracked as 100755" "100755" "${BIN_MODE}" INSTALL_MODE=$(git -C "${REPO_UNDER_TEST}" ls-files -s install.sh | awk '{print $1}') assert_eq "install.sh tracked as 100755" "100755" "${INSTALL_MODE}" # --- Passthrough: real commands reach the real binary transparently -------------- section "Passthrough: real commands reach the real br binary transparently" # Fixture initialized with the REAL binary directly, so passthrough tests do # not depend on any wrapper init behavior. PASSTHRU_PROJ="${WORK}/proj-passthrough" make_project_repo "${PASSTHRU_PROJ}" yes "passthrough-demo" (cd "${PASSTHRU_PROJ}" && "${REAL_BR_RESOLVED}" init -q) WRAPPER_VERSION_OUT=$(br --version) REAL_VERSION_OUT=$("${REAL_BR_RESOLVED}" --version) assert_eq "'br --version' matches real binary output" "${REAL_VERSION_OUT}" "${WRAPPER_VERSION_OUT}" (cd "${PASSTHRU_PROJ}" && br list >/dev/null) pass "'br list' passes through without error" (cd "${PASSTHRU_PROJ}" && br ready >/dev/null) pass "'br ready' passes through without error" JSON_OUT=$(cd "${PASSTHRU_PROJ}" && br list --json) # shellcheck disable=SC2016 # deliberately single-quoted: $1 expands in the inner bash assert_true "'br list --json' output parses as JSON" \ bash -c 'printf "%s" "$1" | python3 -m json.tool >/dev/null' _ "${JSON_OUT}" set +e (cd "${PASSTHRU_PROJ}" && br show definitely-not-a-real-id >/dev/null 2>&1) WRAPPER_EXIT=$? (cd "${PASSTHRU_PROJ}" && "${REAL_BR_RESOLVED}" show definitely-not-a-real-id >/dev/null 2>&1) REAL_EXIT=$? set -e if [[ "${WRAPPER_EXIT}" -ne 0 ]]; then pass "failing command's exit code is nonzero through the wrapper (${WRAPPER_EXIT})" else fail "failing command unexpectedly exited 0 through the wrapper" fi assert_eq "wrapper exit code matches real binary's for same failing invocation" "${REAL_EXIT}" "${WRAPPER_EXIT}" # --- shellcheck (optional, skipped gracefully if unavailable) -------------------- section "shellcheck (optional, skipped gracefully if unavailable)" if command -v shellcheck >/dev/null 2>&1; then for f in "${REPO_UNDER_TEST}/bin/br" "${REPO_UNDER_TEST}/install.sh" "${TESTS_DIR}/run.sh"; do if shellcheck "${f}"; then pass "shellcheck clean: ${f#"${REPO_UNDER_TEST}"/}" else fail "shellcheck reported issues: ${f#"${REPO_UNDER_TEST}"/}" fi done else echo " shellcheck not installed; skipping." fi # --- Summary --------------------------------------------------------------------- section "Summary" echo " passed: ${PASS_COUNT}" echo " failed: ${FAIL_COUNT}" if [[ "${FAIL_COUNT}" -gt 0 ]]; then echo echo "FAILED" exit 1 fi echo echo "ALL PASSED" exit 0 ``` Every later task inserts its sections **immediately before the `# --- shellcheck` block**, keeping shellcheck and Summary last. - [ ] **Step 2: Run the harness to verify it fails** Run: `tests/run.sh` Expected: FAIL — the "installed from local checkout" / "installed version" / "updated 0.0.0 ->" assertions fail because the old `install.sh` doesn't produce them (it also doesn't install anywhere, so the PATH assertion fails too). This proves the new tests bite. - [ ] **Step 3: Rewrite `install.sh` (replaces the entire file)** ```bash #!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail # install.sh — Beads Orphanage installer. # # Normal install (no clone needed): # curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/happycollision/br-orphanage/master/install.sh | bash # # Local dev mode: when run from a checkout of this repo (bin/br sits next to # this script), the local wrapper is copied instead of downloaded, so # contributors and the test harness exercise the local source. # # There is NO auto-update. To update the wrapper, re-run this installer. RAW_BASE="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/happycollision/br-orphanage/master" DATA_DIR="${XDG_DATA_HOME:-${HOME}/.local/share}/br-orphanage" BIN_DIR="${DATA_DIR}/bin" WRAPPER="${BIN_DIR}/br" MARKER="# br-orphanage" LINE="export PATH=\"${BIN_DIR}:\$PATH\" ${MARKER}" wrapper_version() { sed -n 's/^VERSION="\(.*\)"$/\1/p' "$1" | head -n 1 } old_version="" if [[ -f "${WRAPPER}" ]]; then old_version=$(wrapper_version "${WRAPPER}") fi mkdir -p "${BIN_DIR}" # Local dev mode detection: BASH_SOURCE is unset when piped via `curl | bash`. script_dir="" if [[ -n "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" && -f "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" ]]; then script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "$(readlink -f "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")")" && pwd) fi if [[ -n "${script_dir}" && -f "${script_dir}/bin/br" ]]; then cp "${script_dir}/bin/br" "${WRAPPER}" echo "installed from local checkout: ${script_dir}/bin/br" else curl -fsSL "${RAW_BASE}/bin/br" -o "${WRAPPER}" echo "downloaded wrapper from ${RAW_BASE}/bin/br" fi chmod +x "${WRAPPER}" new_version=$(wrapper_version "${WRAPPER}") if [[ -n "${old_version}" && "${old_version}" != "${new_version}" ]]; then echo "br-orphanage: updated ${old_version} -> ${new_version}" else echo "br-orphanage: installed version ${new_version}" fi # Warn (don't fail) if the real br isn't installed yet. found_real=0 IFS=':' read -ra dirs <<< "${PATH}" for d in "${dirs[@]}"; do [[ -x "${d}/br" ]] || continue # shellcheck disable=SC2312 # readlink failure just falls through to "not real" [[ "$(readlink -f "${d}/br")" = "$(readlink -f "${WRAPPER}")" ]] && continue found_real=1 break done if [[ "${found_real}" -eq 0 ]]; then echo "warning: real 'br' binary not found in PATH." echo " Install it: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust" fi # Add the PATH line to whichever shell rc files exist. updated=0 for rc in "${HOME}/.bashrc" "${HOME}/.zshrc"; do [[ -f "${rc}" ]] || continue if grep -qF "${MARKER}" "${rc}"; then echo "already configured: ${rc}" else printf '\n%s\n' "${LINE}" >> "${rc}" echo "updated: ${rc}" updated=1 fi done if [[ "${updated}" -eq 1 ]]; then echo echo "Open a new shell (or 'source' your rc file), then verify:" echo " command -v br # should print ${WRAPPER}" echo " br orphanage --version # wrapper version" fi ``` Note: the current `bin/br` has no `VERSION=` line yet, so `wrapper_version` prints empty and the installer reports `installed version ` (trailing blank). The Task 1 assertions only grep for the substring `installed version`, so the harness passes; Task 2 adds the real VERSION. - [ ] **Step 4: Run the harness to verify it passes** Run: `tests/run.sh` Expected: `ALL PASSED`. (The `updated 0.0.0 ->` upgrade assertion passes because the sed-created `VERSION="0.0.0"` line differs from the empty new version → the installer prints `updated 0.0.0 -> `.) If shellcheck is installed it must be clean on all three files. - [ ] **Step 5: Commit** ```bash git add install.sh tests/run.sh git commit -m "Rewrite installer for curl/local-dev install; new harness skeleton" ``` --- ### Task 2: Wrapper skeleton — `orphanage` namespace, version, transparent passthrough Full rewrite of `bin/br`: legacy interception (`init`/`push`/`restore`) is deleted; `orphanage`/`o` dispatch, `--version`, and usage are added; `init`/`target`/`sync` are stubs replaced in Tasks 3–8. **Files:** - Rewrite: `bin/br` - Modify: `tests/run.sh` (insert new sections before the `# --- shellcheck` block) - [ ] **Step 1: Add the failing tests to `tests/run.sh`** Insert before the `# --- shellcheck` block: ```bash # --- orphanage namespace: version, usage, alias, unknown subcommand ------------- section "br orphanage: version, usage, 'br o' alias, unknown subcommand" SRC_VERSION=$(sed -n 's/^VERSION="\(.*\)"$/\1/p' "${REPO_UNDER_TEST}/bin/br" | head -n 1) if [[ -n "${SRC_VERSION}" ]]; then pass "bin/br declares a VERSION (${SRC_VERSION})" else fail "bin/br has no VERSION= line" fi ORPH_VERSION_OUT=$(br orphanage --version) assert_eq "'br orphanage --version' prints the wrapper version" \ "br-orphanage ${SRC_VERSION}" "${ORPH_VERSION_OUT}" O_VERSION_OUT=$(br o --version) assert_eq "'br o --version' matches 'br orphanage --version'" \ "${ORPH_VERSION_OUT}" "${O_VERSION_OUT}" BARE_ORPH_OUT=$(br orphanage) assert_contains "bare 'br orphanage' prints usage" "${BARE_ORPH_OUT}" "Usage:" assert_contains "bare 'br orphanage' includes the version" "${BARE_ORPH_OUT}" "${SRC_VERSION}" set +e UNKNOWN_OUT=$(br orphanage frobnicate 2>&1) UNKNOWN_EXIT=$? set -e if [[ "${UNKNOWN_EXIT}" -ne 0 ]]; then pass "'br orphanage frobnicate' exits nonzero (${UNKNOWN_EXIT})" else fail "'br orphanage frobnicate' unexpectedly exited 0" fi assert_contains "unknown subcommand names the offender" "${UNKNOWN_OUT}" "unknown subcommand 'frobnicate'" # --- bare 'br init' passes through UNMODIFIED ------------------------------------ section "bare 'br init' passes through to the real binary unmodified" BAREINIT_PROJ="${WORK}/proj-bare-init" make_project_repo "${BAREINIT_PROJ}" yes "bare-init-demo" printf 'node_modules/\n' > "${BAREINIT_PROJ}/.gitignore" (cd "${BAREINIT_PROJ}" && br init -q) assert_dir_exists "real init created .beads/" "${BAREINIT_PROJ}/.beads" BAREINIT_EXCLUDE=$(abs_git_path "${BAREINIT_PROJ}" info/exclude) if grep -qxF '.beads/' "${BAREINIT_EXCLUDE}" 2>/dev/null; then fail "bare 'br init' wrongly added '.beads/' to info/exclude (legacy interception still active)" else pass "bare 'br init' did NOT touch info/exclude" fi # Bare 'br sync' is the real binary's own command: passthrough, no git traffic. (cd "${BAREINIT_PROJ}" && br sync >/dev/null) pass "bare 'br sync' passes through without error" BAREINIT_ORIGIN_REFS=$(git -C "${WORK}/origins/bare-init-demo.git" for-each-ref --format='%(refname)' refs/heads) if [[ "${BAREINIT_ORIGIN_REFS}" == *orphanage* ]]; then fail "bare 'br sync' unexpectedly created an orphan branch at the origin" else pass "bare 'br sync' produced no git traffic (no orphan branch at origin)" fi ``` - [ ] **Step 2: Run the harness to verify the new section fails** Run: `tests/run.sh` Expected: FAIL — no `VERSION=` line, `br orphanage --version` reaches the real binary and errors, and the bare-init test fails because the legacy wrapper still adds the exclude entry. - [ ] **Step 3: Rewrite `bin/br` (replaces the entire file)** ```bash #!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail # br (wrapper) — Beads Orphanage. Shadows the real `br` binary on PATH. # Everything outside the `orphanage` (alias `o`) namespace is exec'd through # to the real binary untouched (exit codes, stdin/stdout, --json, TTY # detection all pass through transparently). Note this includes bare # `br init` and bare `br sync`, which are the real binary's own commands. # # br orphanage init [...] augmented init (+ optional --target ) # br orphanage target [...] show/set this project's sync target # br orphanage sync [--all] converge with the orphan branch # br orphanage --version wrapper version # # Issue data syncs to an ORPHAN BRANCH on a per-project target repo, # configured in the project's own git config (beadsOrphanage.*). See the # design spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-03-br-orphanage-design.md VERSION="0.1.0" SELF=$(readlink -f "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}") # NOTE: further constants (data dir, tracked files, refs, template defaults) # are added by later tasks, in the task that first uses them — defining them # now would fail shellcheck (SC2034 unused) at this task's commit. # --- Find the real br binary (skip ourselves; no recursion) ------------------- find_real_br() { local dir cand local IFS=':' # shellcheck disable=SC2250 # deliberately unbraced/unquoted: word-splits PATH on the IFS=':' set above for dir in $PATH; do [[ -n "${dir}" ]] || continue cand="${dir}/br" [[ -x "${cand}" ]] && [[ -f "${cand}" ]] || continue # shellcheck disable=SC2312 # readlink failure just falls through to "not self" [[ "$(readlink -f "${cand}")" = "${SELF}" ]] && continue printf '%s\n' "${cand}" return 0 done return 1 } # shellcheck disable=SC2310 # failure handled explicitly by the || block below REAL_BR=$(find_real_br) || { echo "br-orphanage: error: real 'br' binary not found in PATH" >&2 echo "br-orphanage: install it first: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust" >&2 exit 1 } # --- Shared helpers ----------------------------------------------------------- usage() { cat <] [args for real 'br init'] br orphanage target [] [--branch

title: Discovery tool for repos with existing orphan branches
description: Brainstorm and build a 'discover' capability for repos that already have their own orphan issue branches (like this one). When you open the same repo on another machine — or even a fresh clone on the same machine — you have to be careful to point the orphanage target at the right place, or you won't get all your old issues back. A discover tool could inspect likely targets (e.g. origin) for existing orphanage branches matching the branch template and offer to adopt them, making bootstrap on a new machine safe and automatic. Needs brainstorming before implementation.

title: Investigate wrapper PATH shadowing: real br wins in some shells
description:
While adding issues via Claude Code (2026-07-03), plain 'br orphanage sync' failed with "unrecognized subcommand 'orphanage'" because the shell resolved br to the real binary, not the wrapper.

Observed on this machine (macOS, zsh):
- 'which br' in a non-interactive shell -> /Users/don.denton/.local/bin/br (the real beads_rust binary)
- The wrapper is correctly installed at ~/.local/share/br-orphanage/bin/br
- Workaround used: invoke the wrapper by full path (~/.local/share/br-orphanage/bin/br orphanage sync), which worked fine

Root cause hypothesis: install.sh adds the wrapper dir to PATH via a marked line in ~/.bashrc / ~/.zshrc, but (a) non-interactive shells (scripts, agent tool calls, cron, CI) don't source those rc files, and/or (b) even when sourced, the line may append the wrapper dir after ~/.local/bin instead of prepending it, so the real br still wins.

Things to investigate / decide:
1. Check the actual PATH ordering in an interactive shell on this machine — is the wrapper actually shadowing br there, or is it silently broken everywhere?
2. Should install.sh prepend the wrapper dir (and verify it lands ahead of common bin dirs like ~/.local/bin, homebrew, cargo)?
3. Should install.sh warn at install time if 'command -v br' after the PATH edit still doesn't resolve to the wrapper?
4. Consider non-interactive coverage: e.g. document the full-path invocation, or add ~/.zshenv support, or install the wrapper as a symlink earlier in an already-first dir.
5. 'br orphanage --version' could double as a health check: a doctor-style check that warns when the wrapper is installed but not first on PATH.

title: Brainstorm: standalone br-orphanage local-dev workflow
description:
Now that br-orphanage is standalone-only, local development should never change what br means. The old concern about clobbering an installed br wrapper is obsolete, but the dev workflow problem remains in standalone form: contributors need an obvious way to run working-tree br-orphanage without overwriting the stable installed command.

Problems to solve:
- Testing dev changes should not overwrite a known-good ~/.local/bin/br-orphanage install unless explicitly requested.
- It should be easy to run the working-tree command, e.g. ./bin/br-orphanage or a repo-local dev bin path.
- The active variant should be visibly distinguishable, likely tied to the dev-version marker issue.

Options to explore:
- Document ./bin/br-orphanage as the default dev invocation.
- Add install.sh --link or a separate dev-install command that links ~/.local/bin/br-orphanage to the working tree with an easy restore.
- Add a repo-local dev bin directory or mise task that prepends the checkout bin path for this repo only.

Acceptance:
- A documented low-friction way to run the working-tree br-orphanage without permanently overwriting the stable install.
- Easy restore to the stable install if a dev link is supported.
- The active variant is visibly distinguishable.

title: Brainstorm: wrap br's doc-producing commands to append an orphanage postscript
description:
Brainstorm whether the wrapper should also intercept br's documentation-producing commands (e.g. 'br robot-docs guide', 'br --help', 'br help', possibly 'br schema') and append a short postscript about the orphanage layer.

Motivation:
- We add an 'orphanage' subcommand whose name is very close to the existing 'br orphans' command. That collision is easy to confuse, and a reader of br's own docs would have no idea the orphanage wrapper exists or how it differs.
- A postscript on the generated docs could: (a) note that 'br orphanage' / 'br o' is a wrapper layer distinct from 'br orphans', (b) point at 'br orphanage --help' for the sync/target workflow, and (c) reduce the confusion between the two similarly-named commands.

Things to brainstorm before building:
1. Which doc commands are worth wrapping — robot-docs guide (agent-facing), human --help/help, schema, all of them?
2. Postscript vs. inline: append after br's output, or something less intrusive?
3. Keep machine output clean — likely suppress the postscript under --json / --format toon / robot flags so we don't corrupt structured output agents parse.
4. Wording that disambiguates 'orphanage' (the wrapper) from 'orphans' (real br) without being noisy on every doc invocation.
5. Whether this belongs only in agent-facing docs (robot-docs) or human help too.

Needs brainstorming before implementation.
close_reason: Obsolete after standalone-only pivot: br-orphanage no longer wraps br documentation commands

title: tests/run.sh: find_real_br_on_path mis-selects the wrapper when it's first on PATH
description:
Discovered while fixing br-orphanage-t9m (2026-07-04).

tests/run.sh locates 'the real br' by taking the FIRST 'br' on the invoker's PATH (find_real_br_on_path, ~line 46). But in a correctly-installed dogfooding shell the WRAPPER is first on PATH (that's the whole point of the install). So REAL_BR resolves to the wrapper, REAL_BR_RESOLVED points at the wrapper, and the Passthrough section — which runs "${REAL_BR_RESOLVED}" init -q expecting the real binary — invokes the wrapper instead and HANGS (observed: run stalled ~5 min on a wrapper 'br init -q', had to be killed).

Workaround used: run the suite with PATH pointed at the real br dir first and the wrapper dir removed:
  env PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:..." tests/run.sh   # -> 142/142 pass

Proposed: find_real_br_on_path should skip any 'br' that is (or resolves to) the br-orphanage wrapper, so it finds the genuine beads_rust binary even when the wrapper shadows it on PATH. Detect the wrapper via its VERSION= / marker, or by comparing readlink -f against the installed wrapper path. Otherwise the suite is unrunnable from a real dogfooding shell without a manual PATH override.
close_reason: find_real_br_on_path now skips wrapper (resolves under br-orphanage data dir); regression test added; suite runs plain 150/0. Fixed on branch install-vs-shell-intercept.

title: br-orphanage sync: announce delegations to real br so its output is attributable
description:
When br-orphanage sync runs, it shells out to the real br binary for sync --flush-only/import-only operations. That real br output is interleaved with br-orphanage output and can be confusing.

Proposal: before each user-facing real-br invocation, print a short attribution line to stderr naming the exact command, e.g. br-orphanage: running real br: br sync --flush-only. Keep stdout focused on br-orphanage result lines.

Call sites in bin/br-orphanage:
- br sync --flush-only in sync_one
- br sync --flush-only --force after inbound merge
- br sync --import-only during inbound merge
- br sync --import-only during bootstrap

Acceptance:
- Every real-br sync/import/flush invocation inside br-orphanage sync is preceded by an attribution line on stderr.
- stdout remains suitable for the wrapper result lines.
- Tests verify stderr contains the attribution and stdout does not.
close_reason: Obsolete: br-orphanage retired; rewritten as git-nook (inner hidden repos, passthrough shim, no sync command). See docs/plans/2026-07-05-git-nook-design.md on branch git-nook.

title: sync: diagnose Git URL rewrites and SSH auth failures
description:
Observed while publishing the standalone-only epic beads: br-orphanage target was configured as origin, git remote -v displayed an HTTPS URL, but bin/br-orphanage sync still failed with SSH/1Password-agent output:

  sign_and_send_pubkey: signing failed for ED25519 "GitHub SSH Key (Generated in 1P)" from agent: communication with agent failed
  git@github.com: Permission denied (publickey).
  br-orphanage: error: could not contact target https://github.com/happycollision/br-orphanage.git

Root cause in this checkout was local Git config interaction: .git/config had remote.origin.url=git@github.com:happycollision/br-orphanage.git and global url.*.insteadOf rules rewrote between HTTPS and SSH. The successful workaround was process-local:

  GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null \
  GIT_CONFIG_COUNT=1 \
  GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0=beadsOrphanage.target \
  GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0=https://github.com/happycollision/br-orphanage.git \
  bin/br-orphanage sync

Problem to solve: when sync fails to contact a target, br-orphanage should make URL resolution and Git rewrite/auth problems diagnosable. Ideas: print the stored target, resolved URL before git operations, and maybe a hint when stderr contains git@github.com while the user-facing resolved URL is HTTPS. Also consider documenting a one-shot literal URL target override or diagnostic command for confusing Git insteadOf setups.

Acceptance:
- A sync contact failure makes clear which target value and resolved URL br-orphanage actually handed to git.
- If Git stderr indicates SSH auth for a target shown as HTTPS, output points toward url.*.insteadOf rewrite rules and SSH agent/auth as likely causes.
- Tests cover a simulated git stderr/auth mismatch or at least the diagnostic formatting around contact failures.
close_reason: Obsolete: br-orphanage retired; rewritten as git-nook (inner hidden repos, passthrough shim, no sync command). See docs/plans/2026-07-05-git-nook-design.md on branch git-nook.

title: Separate install from shell interception
description:
Split install from shell interception, so plain install leaves shell config
untouched and shadowing the real `br` becomes an explicit, print-only opt-in.

## Problem

install.sh currently does two unrelated jobs at once: it copies the `br` wrapper
into ${DATA_DIR}/bin AND edits the user's shell startup files to prepend that dir
to PATH so the wrapper shadows the real `br`. Users who cannot or will not have
their shell config edited have no clean path in.

## Goals

- Install puts the tool on the machine, callable by name, with ZERO shell edits.
- Shell interception becomes an opt-in, advisory `br-orphanage shell-intercept`
  that explains how to shadow `br` and prints a copy-pasteable line — but never
  edits a file.

## Canonical layout

DATA_DIR = ${XDG_DATA_HOME:-~/.local/share}/br-orphanage ; BIN_DIR = $DATA_DIR/bin

- ${BIN_DIR}/br-orphanage — the wrapper, single source of truth.
- ${BIN_DIR}/br — symlink -> br-orphanage. The shadow. Inert until BIN_DIR is
  prepended to PATH ahead of the real `br`.
- A br-orphanage symlink in the first writable dir already on PATH (~/.local/bin,
  /usr/local/bin, ...) so the direct command works immediately with no shell edit.
  Fallback: install prints the full path.

## Wrapper: one file, two personalities (basename "$0")

- Invoked as `br-orphanage` -> orphanage verbs only: init, target, sync,
  shell-intercept, --version, --help. Unknown verb = error, NO passthrough. The
  escape hatch; never shadows anything.
- Invoked as `br` (shadow symlink) -> today's behavior: orphanage/o augmented,
  everything else exec'd to the real `br`.

Shared init/target/sync bodies; only the top-level dispatch differs. Self-detection
(readlink -f skip-self, real-br lookup) unchanged since both names resolve to the
same file.

## shell-intercept (print-only)

Detects shell from $SHELL and prints: what shadowing does; the exact line
(export PATH="${BIN_DIR}:$PATH") and target file — bash -> ~/.bashrc,
zsh -> ~/.zshenv (NOT ~/.zshrc; preserves the br-orphanage-t9m non-interactive
lesson), unknown shell -> generic guidance naming the shell; plus the full-path
fallback. Never edits a file. Reachable as `br-orphanage shell-intercept` and, once
shadowed, `br orphanage shell-intercept`.

## Testing (TDD)

All work is test-driven against tests/run.sh (monolithic bash harness, section /
assert_* helpers, throwaway mktemp fake HOME). Install into a temp HOME + fake
writable PATH dir; assert the symlinks exist and NO shell file is touched; assert
direct mode has no passthrough; assert shadow mode still passes through; assert
shell-intercept names the right file per shell and edits nothing.

## YAGNI

No --apply automation for shell-intercept (print-only by design). No auto-update.

Children are the ordered plan steps; execute in dependency order.
close_reason: All 6 children complete; install/shell-intercept split shipped on branch install-vs-shell-intercept (suite 160/0).

title: install.sh: canonical br-orphanage + inert br shadow, no shell edits
description:
Step 2 of the epic. Rewrite install.sh to the two-step model: canonical
br-orphanage + inert `br` shadow symlink + symlink into first writable PATH dir,
and NO shell edits. Removes all rc-editing/shell-detection from the installer, so
the old Setup assertions that check rc edits are replaced in the same task.
Depends on Task 1.

Files:
- Modify: install.sh (install body)
- Modify: tests/run.sh (Setup section; delete install-side zsh/fish shell tests)

TDD:

STEP 1 (failing tests) — replace the Setup section of tests/run.sh (from
`section "Setup: install.sh..."` through `PATH resolves 'br' to the installed
wrapper`) with:

    section "Setup: install.sh (local dev mode) into fake HOME"
    INSTALL_DIR="${XDG_DATA_HOME}/br-orphanage"
    CANON="${INSTALL_DIR}/bin/br-orphanage"
    SHADOW="${INSTALL_DIR}/bin/br"
    LOCALBIN="${WORK}/localbin"; mkdir -p "${LOCALBIN}"; export PATH="${LOCALBIN}:${PATH}"
    touch "${FAKE_HOME}/.bashrc"; BASHRC_BEFORE=$(cat "${FAKE_HOME}/.bashrc")
    INSTALL_OUT=$("${REPO_UNDER_TEST}/install.sh")
    assert_contains "installer reports local-checkout install" "${INSTALL_OUT}" "installed from local checkout"
    assert_file_exists "canonical wrapper installed" "${CANON}"
    assert_true "canonical wrapper is executable" test -x "${CANON}"
    assert_true "shadow 'br' is a symlink" test -L "${SHADOW}"
    assert_eq "shadow 'br' points at the canonical wrapper" "$(readlink -f "${CANON}")" "$(readlink -f "${SHADOW}")"
    assert_true "br-orphanage symlinked into a writable PATH dir" test -L "${LOCALBIN}/br-orphanage"
    assert_eq "br-orphanage resolves by name to the canonical wrapper" "$(readlink -f "${CANON}")" "$(readlink -f "$(command -v br-orphanage)")"
    assert_contains "installer points user at shell-intercept" "${INSTALL_OUT}" "shell-intercept"
    assert_eq "install left ~/.bashrc untouched" "${BASHRC_BEFORE}" "$(cat "${FAKE_HOME}/.bashrc")"
    assert_file_absent "install wrote no ~/.zshenv" "${FAKE_HOME}/.zshenv"

    section "install.sh: no writable PATH dir falls back to full-path guidance"
    FB_HOME="${WORK}/fallback-home"; mkdir -p "${FB_HOME}"
    FB_OUT=$(env HOME="${FB_HOME}" XDG_DATA_HOME="${FB_HOME}/.local/share" PATH="/usr/bin:/bin" "${REPO_UNDER_TEST}/install.sh")
    assert_contains "fallback names the full path to br-orphanage" "${FB_OUT}" "${FB_HOME}/.local/share/br-orphanage/bin/br-orphanage"
    assert_file_exists "fallback still installs the canonical wrapper" "${FB_HOME}/.local/share/br-orphanage/bin/br-orphanage"

    # upgrade reporting against the canonical file
    if grep -q '^VERSION=' "${CANON}"; then sed -i.bak 's/^VERSION=".*"$/VERSION="0.0.0"/' "${CANON}" && rm -f "${CANON}.bak"; else printf 'VERSION="0.0.0"\n' >> "${CANON}"; fi
    UPGRADE_OUT=$("${REPO_UNDER_TEST}/install.sh")
    assert_contains "upgrade reports old -> new version" "${UPGRADE_OUT}" "updated 0.0.0 ->"

    # shadow mode for the rest of the suite
    export PATH="${INSTALL_DIR}/bin:${REAL_BR_DIR}:${PATH}"
    assert_eq "PATH resolves 'br' to the installed wrapper" "$(readlink -f "${CANON}")" "$(readlink -f "$(command -v br)")"

Delete the now-obsolete install-side sections entirely: "install.sh: non-interactive
zsh resolves 'br' ..." and "install.sh: unrecognized shell gets manual PATH guidance"
(those behaviors move to shell-intercept, Task 4). Grep for remaining INSTALLED_BR and
replace with CANON.

STEP 2: run tests/run.sh, expect FAIL on the shadow-symlink / PATH-dir-symlink /
~/.bashrc-untouched asserts.

STEP 3 (implement) — rewrite install.sh body after the header/vars. Keep RAW_BASE,
DATA_DIR, BIN_DIR, wrapper_version(). Change:
    WRAPPER="${BIN_DIR}/br-orphanage"
    SHADOW="${BIN_DIR}/br"
Drop MARKER/LINE. Source resolution + write unchanged (target is ${WRAPPER}). After
chmod +x and version reporting, replace everything from the old "Warn if real br
missing" through EOF with:

    # Inert shadow: 'br' symlink beside the canonical wrapper.
    ln -sf "br-orphanage" "${SHADOW}"

    find_writable_path_dir() {
        local dir; local IFS=':'
        for dir in $PATH; do
            [[ -n "${dir}" ]] || continue
            [[ "${dir}" == "${BIN_DIR}" ]] && continue
            [[ -d "${dir}" && -w "${dir}" ]] || continue
            printf '%s\n' "${dir}"; return 0
        done
        return 1
    }
    callable_by_name=0
    if target_dir=$(find_writable_path_dir); then
        ln -sf "${WRAPPER}" "${target_dir}/br-orphanage"
        echo "linked: ${target_dir}/br-orphanage -> ${WRAPPER}"
        callable_by_name=1
    fi

    # (keep the existing real-br warning loop, but compare against ${WRAPPER})
    ...found_real loop using readlink -f against "${WRAPPER}"...

    echo
    if [[ "${callable_by_name}" -eq 1 ]]; then
        echo "Installed. 'br-orphanage' is callable now (no shell changes were made)."
    else
        echo "Installed. No writable PATH directory was found, so call it by full path:"
        echo "  ${WRAPPER}"
    fi
    echo
    echo "To make the real 'br' route through this wrapper (optional), run:"
    echo "  br-orphanage shell-intercept"
    echo "It prints exactly what to add to your shell config and changes nothing on its own."

Delete old append_marked_line, the bash/zsh rc-editing blocks, and the user_shell
guidance block.

STEP 4: run tests/run.sh — new Setup section green, downstream shadow-mode green.
STEP 5: commit "install.sh: canonical br-orphanage + inert br shadow, no shell edits"

title: wrapper: $0-based dispatch, direct mode has no passthrough
description:
Step 3 of the epic. Add $0-based dispatch: orphanage verbs only when invoked as
`br-orphanage` (no passthrough), today's behavior when invoked as `br`. Make
REAL_BR resolution lazy so name-only commands (--version, shell-intercept) work
even if the real `br` is absent. Depends on Task 2.

Files:
- Modify: bin/br-orphanage (REAL_BR resolution + dispatch + BIN_DIR)
- Modify: tests/run.sh (new "direct mode" section)

TDD:

STEP 1 (failing tests) — add after the passthrough section. Move the existing
`SRC_VERSION=$(sed -n ...)` assignment UP to just after Setup so both sections use it.

    section "Direct mode: 'br-orphanage' runs orphanage verbs only, no passthrough"
    BRO="${CANON}"   # invoke by canonical name to force direct mode
    assert_eq "br-orphanage --version prints wrapper version" "br-orphanage ${SRC_VERSION}" "$("${BRO}" --version)"
    set +e; UNKNOWN_OUT=$("${BRO}" list 2>&1); UNKNOWN_EXIT=$?; set -e
    assert_eq "br-orphanage <unknown> exits nonzero" "1" "${UNKNOWN_EXIT}"
    assert_contains "br-orphanage <unknown> reports unknown command" "${UNKNOWN_OUT}" "unknown command"
    (cd "${PASSTHRU_PROJ}" && br list >/dev/null); pass "shadow 'br list' still passes through"

STEP 2: run tests/run.sh — expect FAIL (br-orphanage list currently passes through;
--version may error if it hits passthrough).

STEP 3 (implement) in bin/br-orphanage:

a) Lazy REAL_BR. Replace the top-level `REAL_BR=$(find_real_br) || {...exit;}` with:
    REAL_BR=""
    require_real_br() {
        [[ -n "${REAL_BR}" ]] && return 0
        REAL_BR=$(find_real_br) || {
            echo "br-orphanage: error: real 'br' binary not found in PATH" >&2
            echo "br-orphanage: install it first: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust" >&2
            exit 1
        }
    }
   Add `require_real_br` as the first line of cmd_init, cmd_sync, cmd_sync_all, and
   cmd_target (if it invokes the real br). Passthrough calls it before its exec.

b) Add BIN_DIR after SELF=:  BIN_DIR=$(dirname "${SELF}")

c) Add direct-mode usage + dispatcher:
    usage_direct() {
        cat <<EOF
    Beads Orphanage ${VERSION} — direct command (real 'br' is not shadowed)

    Usage:
      br-orphanage init [--target <remote-or-url>] [args for real 'br init']
      br-orphanage target [<remote-or-url>] [--branch <template>] [--namespace <ns>]
      br-orphanage sync [--all]
      br-orphanage shell-intercept
      br-orphanage --version

    Normal issue commands (list, ready, create, ...) are NOT handled here;
    run the real 'br' for those, or shadow it with 'br-orphanage shell-intercept'.
    EOF
    }
    cmd_orphanage_direct() {
        case "${1:-}" in
            init)            shift; cmd_init "$@" ;;
            target)          shift; cmd_target "$@" ;;
            sync)            shift; cmd_sync "$@" ;;
            shell-intercept) shift; cmd_shell_intercept "$@" ;;   # real impl in Task 4
            --version)       echo "br-orphanage ${VERSION}" ;;
            -h|--help|"")    usage_direct ;;
            *) echo "br-orphanage: error: unknown command '${1}'" >&2; usage_direct >&2; exit 1 ;;
        esac
    }

d) Replace the final dispatch case with name-aware dispatch:
    INVOKED_AS=$(basename "$0")
    case "${INVOKED_AS}" in
        br-orphanage) cmd_orphanage_direct "$@" ;;
        *)
            case "${1:-}" in
                orphanage|o) shift; cmd_orphanage "$@" ;;
                *)           require_real_br; exec "${REAL_BR}" "$@" ;;
            esac
            ;;
    esac

NOTE: cmd_shell_intercept doesn't exist until Task 4. Add a temporary stub now:
    cmd_shell_intercept() { echo "not yet implemented" >&2; exit 1; }

STEP 4: run tests/run.sh — direct-mode asserts pass, prior sections still pass.
STEP 5: commit 'wrapper: $0-based dispatch, direct mode has no passthrough'
close_reason: $0-based dispatch: br-orphanage=orphanage-only (no passthrough), br=shadow passthrough; lazy REAL_BR; suite 151/0

title: wrapper: print-only shell-intercept guidance
description:
Step 4 of the epic. Implement cmd_shell_intercept: print-only, shell-aware
guidance for shadowing `br`. Expose under both modes. Replaces the Task 3 stub.
Depends on Task 3.

Files:
- Modify: bin/br-orphanage (cmd_shell_intercept + wire into cmd_orphanage)
- Modify: tests/run.sh (new "shell-intercept" section)

TDD:

STEP 1 (failing tests):
    section "shell-intercept: prints per-shell guidance, edits nothing"
    BRO="${CANON}"
    EXPORT_LINE="export PATH=\"${INSTALL_DIR}/bin:\$PATH\""

    SI_BASH_HOME="${WORK}/si-bash"; mkdir -p "${SI_BASH_HOME}"
    BASH_OUT=$(env HOME="${SI_BASH_HOME}" SHELL=/bin/bash "${BRO}" shell-intercept)
    assert_contains "bash guidance names ~/.bashrc" "${BASH_OUT}" ".bashrc"
    assert_contains "bash guidance gives the export line" "${BASH_OUT}" "${EXPORT_LINE}"
    assert_contains "bash guidance offers the full-path fallback" "${BASH_OUT}" "${INSTALL_DIR}/bin/br-orphanage"
    assert_file_absent "shell-intercept wrote no ~/.bashrc" "${SI_BASH_HOME}/.bashrc"

    ZSH_OUT=$(env SHELL=/bin/zsh "${BRO}" shell-intercept)
    assert_contains "zsh guidance names ~/.zshenv" "${ZSH_OUT}" ".zshenv"
    assert_true "zsh guidance does not steer to .zshrc" bash -c '! grep -q "\.zshrc" <<<"$1"' _ "${ZSH_OUT}"

    FISH_OUT=$(env SHELL=/opt/fish "${BRO}" shell-intercept)
    assert_contains "unknown-shell guidance names the shell" "${FISH_OUT}" "fish"
    assert_contains "unknown-shell guidance still gives the export line" "${FISH_OUT}" "${EXPORT_LINE}"

    SHADOW_SI=$(br orphanage shell-intercept)
    assert_contains "'br orphanage shell-intercept' works in shadow mode" "${SHADOW_SI}" "${EXPORT_LINE}"

STEP 2: run tests/run.sh — expect FAIL (stub / not wired into cmd_orphanage).

STEP 3 (implement) — replace the Task-3 stub with:

    cmd_shell_intercept() {
        local shell_name line
        shell_name=$(basename "${SHELL:-}" 2>/dev/null || true)
        line="export PATH=\"${BIN_DIR}:\$PATH\""
        cat <<EOF
    Shadowing makes the real 'br' route through this wrapper by putting

      ${BIN_DIR}

    first on your PATH. After that, 'br orphanage ...' is augmented and every
    other 'br' command passes through to the real binary untouched.

    EOF
        case "${shell_name}" in
            bash) printf 'Detected bash. Add this line to ~/.bashrc:\n\n  %s\n\n' "${line}" ;;
            zsh)
                printf 'Detected zsh. Add this line to ~/.zshenv — NOT ~/.zshrc, so that\n'
                printf 'non-interactive zsh (scripts, agents, cron) sees it too:\n\n  %s\n\n' "${line}" ;;
            "") printf 'Could not detect your shell. Add this line to its startup file:\n\n  %s\n\n' "${line}" ;;
            *)
                printf 'Detected %s, which this tool does not configure automatically.\n' "${shell_name}"
                printf 'Add the equivalent of this to its startup file:\n\n  %s\n\n' "${line}" ;;
        esac
        cat <<EOF
    Nothing was changed — copy the line above yourself.

    Prefer no PATH changes? Skip shadowing and call the wrapper directly:
      ${BIN_DIR}/br-orphanage sync
    EOF
    }

Also add to cmd_orphanage (shadow-mode namespace) so `br orphanage shell-intercept`
works:
    shell-intercept) shift; cmd_shell_intercept "$@" ;;

STEP 4: run tests/run.sh — all shell-intercept asserts pass; full suite green.
STEP 5: commit 'wrapper: print-only shell-intercept guidance'
close_reason: Implemented print-only shell-intercept (both modes); shell-aware, edits nothing; suite 160/0

title: Regression run + beads sync + orphan-branch verify
description:
Step 6 of the epic. Full-suite regression + close the epic + orphan sync + verify.
Depends on Task 5.

Steps:

title: br-orphanage --version: mark dev/working-tree installs
description:
br-orphanage --version currently prints a bare VERSION string. When a development or working-tree command is active instead of a stable installed copy, there is no way to tell from version output which variant is running.

Design questions:
- How should br-orphanage know it is a dev/local command? Candidates include resolved path inside a git checkout, an install.sh --link marker, or a generated/stamped version suffix for dev installs.
- A stable install copied from a repo should keep reporting the plain version.
- The choice should line up with the standalone local-dev workflow issue.

Acceptance:
- Stable installs print the plain version, e.g. br-orphanage 0.1.0.
- Dev/working-tree invocations print a clearly marked version, e.g. br-orphanage 0.1.0+dev.
- The marking mechanism is documented alongside the dev workflow.

title: sync: spurious 'both changed config.yaml' conflict when local and remote are identical
description:
'br orphanage sync' warns about a config.yaml conflict — and preserves the remote blob — even when the local and remote config.yaml are byte-for-byte identical. This looks like it will cause needless thrashing on every sync.

Observed (2026-07-03, fresh install, first syncs of br-orphanage):
Warning emitted:
  br-orphanage: warning: both local and remote changed config.yaml; kept local.
  Remote version preserved at 99c607c09cd12392e454e944559b758e1477e25c — view it with:
  git cat-file blob 99c607c09cd12392e454e944559b758e1477e25c:config.yaml

But the local and remote config.yaml were exactly the same content. There is no real conflict to resolve, yet the wrapper reports one and stashes a 'preserved' blob.

Why this matters:
- A no-op sync should be silent about config.yaml. Flagging identical files as a conflict trains users to ignore the warning, and risks repeated 'kept local' churn on every sync.
- If the three-way merge keys off 'both sides touched the file since the base' rather than 'the sides actually differ', identical edits will always trip it.

Proposed:
1. Before warning, compare the actual content/hash of local vs remote config.yaml. If they're identical, treat it as no conflict — no warning, no preserved blob.
2. Only warn when local and remote content genuinely differ.
3. Confirm this doesn't leave the base/ancestor pointer stale in a way that re-triggers the false conflict next sync.

title: init: guide user to set a target after 'br orphanage init'
description:
When running 'br orphanage init' without a target, init completes but gives no indication of what the target was set to — turns out it wasn't set at all, and there was no message telling the user to select a target.

Observed (2026-07-03, fresh install of br + br-orphanage):
- Ran 'br orphanage init' with no --target
- Init reported success, but the orphanage target was left unconfigured
- Nothing in the output signaled this, or pointed at 'br orphanage target <remote-or-url>' as the next step

Not supplying a target isn't itself a problem — leaving it unset is a legitimate state. The issue is the silence. A user can't tell configured-with-target from configured-without-target from the init output.

Proposed:
1. After init, print the resolved target state explicitly (e.g. 'Orphanage target: <value>' or 'Orphanage target: not set').
2. When no target was supplied, add a short next-step hint pointing at 'br orphanage target <remote-or-url>' to select one, and note that sync needs a target.
3. Consider a 'br orphanage target' with no args printing the current target so users can check state at any time.

title: Protect against issue_prefix drift across machines/clones (default = dir name)
description:
Defend against issue_prefix drift — but ONLY within what we control, the 'br orphanage|o' wrapper. Everything about how br proper generates prefixes/IDs is upstream and out of scope here; we can detect and pin, not change br's defaults.

## Severity: cosmetic/traceability, NOT functional (verified 2026-07-04)
Mixed prefixes do not break br. Verified empirically in an isolated DB: with issues under two different prefixes, `br list`, `br dep add` (cross-prefix), `br dep tree`, `br show`, and `br ready` all work correctly — including blocking semantics taking effect across the prefix boundary (a bbb-* issue blocked by an open aaa-* issue was correctly hidden from `br ready`). IDs are opaque `<prefix>-<hash>` strings; the hash provides uniqueness, and nothing downstream filters/groups/validates by prefix. `issue_prefix` ONLY controls the minting of NEW IDs (br create/q). So this issue is about consistency and provenance ("which project owns this ID"), not correctness — de-prioritize accordingly; it will not break queries, deps, ready-work, sync, or orphan-branch mechanics. The real footgun is that the per-machine dir-name default silently decides the prefix when issue_prefix is left commented out.

## What happened (context)
br proper's default issue_prefix is the repo's directory name. This project was first created on another machine under the dir 'beads-sync', so those issues got 'beads-sync-*' IDs; on this machine the dir is 'br-orphanage', so newer issues got 'br-orphanage-*'. .beads/config.yaml ships with issue_prefix commented out, so nothing pins it and the per-machine dir-name default silently wins. Result was a mixed-prefix DB, hand-repaired 2026-07-04.

## Source of truth for the canonical prefix
The orphan branch's config.yaml also has issue_prefix commented out, so it does NOT pin identity either. The reliable signal is the ISSUE IDs already present on the orphanage branch (here: consistently 'br-orphanage-*'). Derive the canonical prefix from those, not from config.

## Wrapper-scoped work (what we can actually do)
1. During 'br orphanage' init / discover / attach (see br-orphanage-lp0), read the issues on the orphanage branch and derive the canonical prefix from their IDs.
2. Compare to the local effective prefix ('br config get issue_prefix'). If they differ, warn loudly.
3. Pin it: write .beads/config.yaml locally with issue_prefix uncommented and set to the canonical value, so the dir-name default can never silently take over on this clone. (The commented '# issue_prefix: br-orphanage' hint is what saved us — keep pinning explicit.)
4. Also write the pinned prefix into the config.yaml the wrapper commits to the orphanage branch, so future clones inherit a pinned value, not just a comment.
5. On 'br orphanage sync', detect a mixed-prefix DB (issues under >1 prefix) and surface it as a warning, pointing at the manual repair path below. (Actually rewriting IDs + rebuilding the DB is a br-proper operation, so we detect/warn/document rather than auto-fix.)

## Manual repair we performed (reference)
No deps/blocks/comments referenced any ID, so a straight rewrite was safe: rewrote the leading "id" field of each 'beads-sync-*' line in .beads/issues.jsonl to 'br-orphanage-*' (kept the hash suffix), cleaned one compound ID to 'br-orphanage-rxy', left title text untouched, then rebuilt clean (rm .beads/beads.db*; br sync --import-only). Verified with br list --all and br doctor health.

## Acceptance
- A clone/checkout under a different dir name than the canonical prefix does NOT silently mint wrong-prefixed IDs — the wrapper pins the prefix from the orphanage branch's existing IDs.
- The pin is written to BOTH the local .beads/config.yaml and the copy committed to the orphanage branch.
- A mixed-prefix DB is detected and reported by 'br orphanage sync', with the manual repair path documented.

Related: br-orphanage-lp0 (discovery).

comments.author: dondenton
comments.text:
Observed while syncing after the manual repair (br-orphanage @ 2026-07-04):

CRUCIAL: the config.yaml stored ON THE ORPHAN BRANCH also has issue_prefix COMMENTED OUT (identical to the local one). So the orphanage branch's config file does NOT pin project identity either. This breaks the naive 'read the canonical prefix from the remote/orphanage config' plan.

Implication for the fix: the true source of truth for a project's canonical prefix is the ISSUE IDs already on the orphan branch (all 'br-orphanage-*'), NOT config.yaml. The reconciler should:
  1. Derive the canonical prefix from the IDs present on the orphanage branch (majority/consistent prefix of existing issues).
  2. Then pin it locally by writing/uncommenting issue_prefix in .beads/config.yaml.
  3. AND fix the branch's own config.yaml so future clones have a pinned value, not just the commented hint.

Minor side-observation (not blocking): after 'br orphanage sync', origin correctly received the new commit (0613237), but the local remote-tracking ref refs/remotes/origin/orphanage/... stayed stale at the previous commit — the wrapper pushes directly without updating the tracking ref. Cosmetic, but could confuse tooling that inspects remote-tracking refs to decide sync state.

Also: 'br orphanage sync' printed 'Nothing to export (no dirty issues)' even though a brand-new issue was created just before — the create auto-flushed to JSONL, so the sync's flush step correctly found nothing, but it still committed a new tree. The messaging reads as a no-op when it wasn't. Worth softening.

comments.author: dondenton
comments.text: Scope note (supersedes parts of the earlier comment): dropping everything that isn't ours. The 'br sync' no-op-message observation and any br-proper default/doctor behavior are OUT of scope and are NOT being filed — we don't yet understand the message and it isn't ours to change. This issue is strictly about the 'br orphanage' wrapper detecting prefix drift and pinning config.

title: Design: make br-orphanage standalone-only and abandon br wrapping
description:
Decision/design
Make br-orphanage a standalone companion command only. The supported command surface becomes:

  br-orphanage init [--target <remote-or-url>] [args for real br init]
  br-orphanage target [<remote-or-url>] [--branch <template>] [--namespace <ns>]
  br-orphanage sync [--all]
  br-orphanage --version
  br-orphanage --help

Normal issue work remains the real br: br ready, br list, br create, br close, etc. br-orphanage may invoke the real br internally for br init and br sync --flush-only/import-only, but it must never pretend to be br.

Rejected alternatives
1. Keep optional PATH shadowing: rejected because it creates hidden local state, ambiguous agent behavior, passthrough compatibility burden, and confusing failure attribution.
2. Rename primary command to bro: rejected because it is cute but ambiguous, less searchable, and weaker for agents than br-orphanage. Users can create their own alias later, but docs/tests should not depend on it.
3. Keep br orphanage/br o as compatibility aliases: rejected for this redesign. A clean break is simpler while the project is still young, and avoids carrying wrapper semantics indefinitely.

Human usage goal
The install path should be boring: install br-orphanage, then run br-orphanage sync. No shell startup files, no PATH shadow instructions, no command that changes what br means.

Agent usage goal
Instructions should be explicit: use real br for issue CRUD, use br-orphanage only for orphan-branch sync/target/init. Agent docs must stop saying br o sync or br orphanage sync. This reduces accidental dependence on user shell state.

Implementation shape
Remove the shadow symlink, $0 dispatch, shell-intercept command, passthrough tests, passthrough docs, and all br orphanage/br o guidance. Preserve the sync engine, target config, augmented init, bootstrap behavior, and machine-local project-paths index.

Testing shape
Tests should exercise the installed br-orphanage command directly. They should still use the real br binary for fixtures and for verifying internal delegation, but no test should prepend the install bin as a br shadow or assert passthrough behavior.

Completion signal
rg over README.md AGENTS.md bin/br-orphanage install.sh tests/run.sh should find no user-facing references to shell-intercept, passthrough, shadowing, br orphanage, or br o, except historical issue text in .beads. tests/run.sh passes. br-orphanage sync is used to publish the updated beads.
close_reason: Completed standalone-only CLI pivot; tests/run.sh passes and stale wrapper guidance is removed

title: Adopt direct user-bin install and release verification conventions
description:
Goal
Update the standalone-only redesign so install behaves like a normal CLI installer and has a credible release-integrity story. This refines the installer task before implementation.

Current convention research
Well-regarded curl installers generally install a standalone executable into a user bin path or a tool-owned bin path, with an override:
- uv documents a standalone installer and removes binaries from ~/.local/bin on uninstall.
- mise defaults MISE_INSTALL_PATH to ~/.local/bin/mise and supports overriding the binary path.
- Deno installs to a tool-owned bin dir configurable via DENO_INSTALL.
- rustup remains the classic curl | sh flow with TLS flags and interactive guidance.

Decision for br-orphanage
- Install the executable directly as br-orphanage in a user binary path, preferably ~/.local/bin/br-orphanage by default.
- Support an override such as BR_ORPHANAGE_INSTALL_PATH=/some/path/br-orphanage.
- Do not install any br symlink, shadow shim, shell-intercept hook, or passthrough mechanism.
- If ~/.local/bin is missing, create it when reasonable.
- If the chosen install dir is not on PATH, print focused PATH guidance for br-orphanage only.
- Keep data/cache state separate from the executable path; only executable installation belongs in ~/.local/bin.

Dev workflow implication
Once stable installs do not manipulate br, PATH manipulation can belong exclusively to local development:
- Stable: br-orphanage resolves to ~/.local/bin/br-orphanage or the chosen install path.
- Dev: prepend a repo/dev bin path, or run ./bin/br-orphanage directly, so br-orphanage resolves to the working tree.
- Never change what br means in either stable or dev mode.

Security and release-integrity requirements
- Release assets should include the br-orphanage executable and a SHA256 checksum file.
- The installer should verify the downloaded executable against its checksum when installing from a release asset. This protects against corrupt downloads or asset mismatch, but not against a compromised repo/release account if the checksum is replaced too.
- Document a cautious install path: download install.sh, inspect it, then run it.
- Add a follow-up release-hardening path for GitHub artifact attestations or Sigstore/cosign verification. These provide a stronger independent provenance signal than a same-origin checksum.
- Prefer GitHub artifact attestations for this project once release automation exists, because users can verify provenance with gh attestation verify ... -R happycollision/br-orphanage.
- Consider immutable GitHub releases once release assets are attached, so published assets cannot be silently replaced.

Implementation relationship
This task should be completed before the installer rewrite task. The installer rewrite should absorb this decision instead of preserving the current ~/.local/share/br-orphanage/bin plus PATH symlink model.

Acceptance
- README install section says the normal install target is ~/.local/bin/br-orphanage and documents BR_ORPHANAGE_INSTALL_PATH or the chosen override variable.
- install.sh installs the executable directly at the selected install path.
- install.sh output contains no br shadowing language and no tool-owned bin shim unless explicitly justified.
- tests cover default install path, override install path, PATH guidance when install dir is not on PATH, and absence of any br symlink.
- Release/security docs explain the limit of checksums and name attestations/signatures as the stronger protection against distribution compromise.
close_reason: Adopted direct user-bin install path, override, and release-integrity documentation

title: Investigate wrapper PATH shadowing: real br wins in some shells
description:
While adding issues via Claude Code (2026-07-03), plain 'br orphanage sync' failed with "unrecognized subcommand 'orphanage'" because the shell resolved br to the real binary, not the wrapper.

Observed on this machine (macOS, zsh):
- 'which br' in a non-interactive shell -> /Users/don.denton/.local/bin/br (the real beads_rust binary)
- The wrapper is correctly installed at ~/.local/share/br-orphanage/bin/br
- Workaround used: invoke the wrapper by full path (~/.local/share/br-orphanage/bin/br orphanage sync), which worked fine

Root cause hypothesis: install.sh adds the wrapper dir to PATH via a marked line in ~/.bashrc / ~/.zshrc, but (a) non-interactive shells (scripts, agent tool calls, cron, CI) don't source those rc files, and/or (b) even when sourced, the line may append the wrapper dir after ~/.local/bin instead of prepending it, so the real br still wins.

Things to investigate / decide:
1. Check the actual PATH ordering in an interactive shell on this machine — is the wrapper actually shadowing br there, or is it silently broken everywhere?
2. Should install.sh prepend the wrapper dir (and verify it lands ahead of common bin dirs like ~/.local/bin, homebrew, cargo)?
3. Should install.sh warn at install time if 'command -v br' after the PATH edit still doesn't resolve to the wrapper?
4. Consider non-interactive coverage: e.g. document the full-path invocation, or add ~/.zshenv support, or install the wrapper as a symlink earlier in an already-first dir.
5. 'br orphanage --version' could double as a health check: a doctor-style check that warns when the wrapper is installed but not first on PATH.
design:
Root cause: install.sh wrote the PATH-prepend line only to ~/.bashrc and ~/.zshrc. zsh sources ~/.zshrc for INTERACTIVE shells only; non-interactive zsh (agent tool calls, scripts, cron, CI) sources ~/.zshenv, which had no marker -> real br at ~/.local/bin shadowed the wrapper. Hypothesis (b) 'wrong ordering' was disproved: the line correctly prepends (wrapper is #1 on PATH in interactive login zsh). Only hypothesis (a) held.

Fix (install.sh): target ~/.zshenv for zsh (the file zsh sources for EVERY invocation), creating it when the user uses zsh (existing ~/.zshenv/~/.zshrc, or $SHELL=zsh). bash-only users get no ~/.zshenv. Interactive zsh keeps working (it sources ~/.zshenv before ~/.zshrc).
